detailed_analysis: src/agents_sync/tool_specs/opencode.py is a single declarative spec-factory module (Layer 3 'wiring', per architecture §4/§7). It defines one public function, build_opencode_spec, which lazily imports the opencode IO adapters, defines six thin closures adapting the opencode_io functions to the generic CustomizationTypeIO signatures, and returns one AgenticToolSpec literal. The file is 153 lines (well under the 300-line module limit) and contains no I/O, no secrets, no injection surface, no global mutable state, no silent exception swallowing, and no unsafe deserialization. Security posture is clean: the MCP-server secret_policy is delegated to build_mcp_server_io, which the architecture (goal 6 / NFR-15) makes responsible for egress secret handling — this file correctly does not re-implement it. The Dependency Rule holds: every import points inward (agentic_tool_spec port, opencode_io adapter, shared _mcp_server_factory / _rules_factory helpers); the concrete name 'opencode' appearing here is explicitly sanctioned by architecture §1 ('the names ... appear in adapter modules and in user-provided config keys'). The principal structural observation is that build_opencode_spec is ~137 lines, over the 40-line guideline; however the body is almost entirely nested micro-closures plus a declarative dataclass literal, and this exact shape is the project-wide convention for every tool_specs/*.py factory (codex.py is structurally identical). The notable inconsistency is API style: this file uses the legacy storage=/file_suffix=/slugify_name=/reserved_names= fields on CustomizationTypeIO, whereas codex.py uses the newer typed file_layout=DirectorySkillLayout()/SharedKeyedMapLayout(). Both are valid (CustomizationTypeIO.__post_init__ accepts either), but the divergence is a maintainability smell within a directory of sibling factories.

dependency_graph_summary.note: Imports point inward only: agentic_tool_spec (port), opencode_io (adapter), _mcp_server_factory, _rules_factory, slash_command_io, mcp_server_io. Dependency Rule (architecture §3) upheld.

findings.problem: build_opencode_spec spans ~137 lines, exceeding the project's 40-line function guideline (AGENTS.md §3). Cyclomatic complexity is 1 (no branching) and the body is overwhelmingly six small wrapper closures plus a single declarative AgenticToolSpec literal, so cognitive load is low and this is the established convention for every tool_specs/*.py factory. Flagged for completeness against the explicit line limit; severity reduced because the content is declarative wiring, not logic.

findings.variant: Inconsistent API style across sibling factories
findings.problem: This factory configures CustomizationTypeIO via the legacy untyped fields storage=/file_suffix=/slugify_name=/reserved_names=, whereas the sibling codex.py uses the newer typed layout objects file_layout=DirectorySkillLayout() and SharedKeyedMapLayout(...). Both are accepted by CustomizationTypeIO.__post_init__, but the divergence within one directory of parallel factories raises maintenance cost and risks the two code paths drifting (the typed-layout path carries probe_check_path / tolerates_missing_config_key polymorphism the legacy path lacks). Not a correctness defect; a consistency/maintainability smell.

The closures accept ParseFn-protocol keyword parameters (artifact_path, artifact_root) that they then silently drop because the underlying opencode_io parsers do not consume them. This is required to satisfy the ParseFn Protocol signature, so it is intentional adapter shimming rather than a true defect, but the discarded keywords are quietly load-bearing-by-absence: a reader cannot tell whether the omission is deliberate or a bug. Low severity, noted for clarity.

verdict_summary: Clean declarative Layer-3 spec factory; no critical/major findings, Dependency Rule and NFR-15 delegation upheld; only minor style/consistency smells.
